prompt: Edit this studio worktable photo into a genuinely lived-in, mid-work mess. The printed cotton is loosely crumpled rather than neatly draped. Add scattered fabric offcuts and a selvedge strip, a tomato pincushion bristling with pins, a cloth tape measure trailing off the table edge, tailor's chalk and a worn wooden ruler, a few stray threads catching the light, and a half-finished cup of tea leaving a faint ring on the wood. No hands, no people. Keep the warm honey Mediterranean grade and soft directional window daylight of the source. Natural unfinished-wood worktable. The hero fabric is Me + Lia's small blue-and-white floral printed cotton. Make it look like a real, candid photograph of a working dressmaking atelier mid-task, not a styled stock flatlay. 35mm film look, gentle natural grain, realistic shallow depth of field, authentic asymmetry and imperfection. No text, no logos, no watermarks. Portrait framing.
